name: A royal priesthood
notes: In 1 Peter 2:9 the apostle Peter gathers up Israel's ancient covenant titles and lays them upon the church. The shared core is the cluster of designations God gave His people at Sinai and through the prophets: a priestly kingdom, an holy nation, a chosen and formed people who exist to declare His praise. Exodus 19:6 supplies 'an holy nation' and the priestly-kingly office; Isaiah 43:20-21 supplies the chosen people formed by God to 'shew forth' His praise; Deuteronomy 7:6 supplies the picture of a people set apart and chosen as His own treasured possession. Each Old Testament passage keeps its own setting -- Sinai's covenant charter, the prophet's promise of restoration, and Moses' sermon on Israel's election -- while Peter weaves them into a single confession for those called out of darkness into His marvellous light.
